title: Shattering Kicked Turkish Cornmeal Kofte
description: Pungent, shattering Turkish dish made with white cornmeal for post-workout fuel, featuring a spicy kick and a mother sauce base. Perfect for low-carb diets and a quick weeknight meal.
instructions: Combine cornmeal, lamb, mother sauce base, paprika, cayenne pepper, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper in a large bowl.
Mix well until everything is fully incorporated.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Using your hands, shape the mixture into 8-10 kofte, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
Place the kofte on the grill and cook for 4-5 minutes per side, or until they are nicely charred and cooked through.
Transfer the kofte to a plate and garnish with parsley.
Note: You can also bake the kofte in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 12-15 minutes, or until cooked through.
Serve with a side of tzatziki sauce or a green salad for a well-rounded meal.
If desired, add a dollop of spicy harissa on top of the kofte for an extra kick.
